description: This dual-Y-axis plot (Fig. 4) reports the variation of diameter and volume fraction of the Fe3Al ordered phase as a function of Al content in Fe-Al based ferritic low-density steels. The horizontal axis indicates weight percentage of Al at discrete levels of 5, 6, 7, and 9 wt%. Solid circles (left Y-axis, Volume fraction, 0–3 %) represent the volume fraction of the ordered phase, while solid squares (right Y-axis, Diameter, 0–4 nm) denote its average diameter, both determined from small-angle X-ray scattering (SAXS) analysis with associated error bars. The volume fraction increases monotonically from approximately 0.4 % at 5 wt% Al to about 1.9 % at 9 wt% Al. Concurrently, the ordered-phase diameter rises from roughly 2.6 nm to about 3.7 nm across the same compositional range. These trends quantitatively demonstrate that increasing Al content promotes greater precipitation of nano-sized ordered Fe3Al (DO3) domains, supporting the core conclusion that ordered-phase strengthening serves as an additional strengthening mechanism in these alloys, consistent with accompanying TEM and Mössbauer characterization.
